headline: Models nail Apple surge while oil predictions crash spectacularly
summary: AI models showed strong consensus on major indices and Apple, correctly predicting upward movement as AAPL soared past $280 on earnings beats. However, energy sector predictions were catastrophically wrong, with XLE plummeting to $58.85 despite unanimous bullish sentiment on oil prices above $100.

consensus_picks.note: All five models correctly predicted SPY's rise to $720.65, showing strong consensus on market direction.
Three models correctly called Apple's surge to $280.14 following earnings beat, while Gemini incorrectly predicted a decline.
GPT-4o and Perplexity correctly predicted QQQ's rise while Claude wrongly called for a decline despite Apple's strength.
Both models incorrectly predicted Bitcoin's decline, missing its rise to $78,169.

best_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence Apple call with 85% certainty perfectly captured the earnings-driven surge past $280.

worst_call.summary: Claude's confident energy sector bet failed catastrophically as XLE crashed to $58.85 despite oil price reasoning.
